title: striga alba
intro: Striga alba, also known as White Witchweed, is a parasitic plant that belongs to the Orobanchaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving headaches, and soothing digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Striga alba can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be cautioned due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
